pr.title: Interface related renames, include cleanup, removal of "un" directory
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Moved the remaining `src/melee/un` units into the interface subsystem, split the old monolithic `un_2FC9.h` declarations into focused `if` headers, migrated `un/types.h` contents into `if/types.h`, updated build/split metadata, and cleaned include users across db/gm/lb/mn/ty/gr code. The change was primarily a repository organization and naming cleanup: code formerly tracked as unknown interface-adjacent files now lives under `melee/if` as `if_2FC93`, `ifprize`, `ifcoget`, `soundtest`, `textdraw`, and `textlib`. The automated match report showed path-based movement of 97 matches and 39 unmatched-function improvements/regressions from `main/melee/un/*` to `main/melee/if/*`, consistent with file renames rather than semantic decomp changes.
what_changed: Removed the standalone `MeleeLib("un (Unknown)")` block from `configure.py` and inserted its six objects into the existing interface library block as `melee/if/if_2FC93.c`, `ifprize.c`, `ifcoget.c`, `soundtest.c`, `textdraw.c`, and `textlib.c`.
Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so the same address ranges previously named `melee/un/un_2FC93.c` through `melee/un/un_3028.c` are now named under `melee/if/*`.

Renamed unknown files into interface-oriented names: `un_2FC94.c` became `ifprize.c`, `un_2FC95.c` became `ifcoget.c`, `un_2FC96.c` became `soundtest.c`, `un_2FC97.c` became `textdraw.c`, and `un_3028.c` became `textlib.c`; `un_2FC93.c` became `if_2FC93.c` while retaining its address-like placeholder name.

Replaced broad includes of `un/un_2FC9.h`, `un/un_3028.h`, and `un/types.h` with narrower interface headers such as `if/textdraw.h`, `if/textlib.h`, `if/ifcoget.h`, `if/ifprize.h`, `if/soundtest.h`, and `if/types.h`.

Deleted `src/melee/un/forward.h` and `src/melee/un/types.h`; moved `DevText`, `DEVTEXT_FLAG_*`, `un_804D6EF4_t`, `un_80301C64_t`, and `un_80304138_objalloc_t*` definitions/forward declarations into `src/melee/if/types.h` and `src/melee/if/forward.h`.
Created focused public headers for moved interface units, including `if_2FC93.h`, `ifcoget.h`, `ifprize.h`, and `textdraw.h`, preserving address comments on declarations.
Adjusted many include blocks in db/gm/lb/mn/ty/gr files to prefer local project includes and to include the specific interface dependency directly rather than relying on old unknown umbrella headers.

Made minor type/include cleanup alongside the renames, such as changing `un_802FED10_OnLeave(UNK_T unused)` to `un_802FED10_OnLeave(void* unused)` and using `un_80304138_objalloc_t*` after moving the typedef into `if/forward.h`.
smart_moves: Kept the address-based function names and comments while moving files, which preserved searchable decomp provenance even though the source units were reclassified from `un` to `if`.
Updated all three coordination points together: source paths, `configure.py` object lists, and `config/GALE01/splits.txt`. That avoids stale build/split metadata after a large file move.
Converted the old umbrella `un_2FC9.h` into smaller headers by responsibility, reducing accidental dependencies and making future function ownership clearer.
Moved shared text/debug display structs into `if/types.h` and forwards into `if/forward.h`, letting users include type information without retaining an obsolete `un` subsystem.
Used semantic file names where evidence was strong (`soundtest`, `textdraw`, `textlib`, `ifprize`, `ifcoget`) but left `if_2FC93` address-like instead of inventing a likely-wrong descriptive name.
decomp_lessons: When retiring an `un` directory, rename files only after updating build config and split labels; otherwise automated match reports will look like wholesale broken/new matches even if code is unchanged.

Large PRs that are mostly path renames can still improve matching visibility: the bot reported 97 new matches and 39 improvements under `main/melee/if/*`, mirrored by equivalent losses under old `main/melee/un/*` names.
Prefer focused headers per source unit over a broad unknown umbrella header; this makes include ownership easier to audit and helps future decomp work identify subsystem boundaries.
Move structs with their true subsystem once enough context is known. `DevText` and related object allocator types belong with interface text rendering, not an unknown namespace.
Preserve address comments on declarations during renames. They provide continuity for old search terms and help verify that only ownership changed.
Do not over-name unknown functions during file moves. This PR retained many `un_80...` function names while improving file/module names, a safer intermediate step.
naming_conventions: `src/melee/un/*` was eliminated in favor of `src/melee/if/*` for interface-related code.
`un_2FC94` was renamed to `ifprize`, indicating prize/trophy interface ownership.
`un_2FC95` was renamed to `ifcoget`, indicating Continue/Get interface ownership from available naming evidence.
`un_2FC96` was renamed to `soundtest`, matching its sound test declarations and includes.
`un_2FC97` was renamed to `textdraw`, containing `DevText_*` drawing/setup functions.
`un_3028` was renamed to `textlib`, containing `DevText_Print`, `DevText_Printf`, and related text library helpers.
Header guards for new interface headers used simple subsystem names such as `_IFCOGET_H_`, `_IFPRIZE_H_`, `_SOUNDTEST_H_`, and `_TEXTDRAW_H_`.
Type names already present as unknowns, such as `un_80304138_objalloc_t`, were preserved rather than force-renamed without stronger evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR mostly performed file moves and include cleanup rather than direct assembly matching rewrites.
The decomp bot reported `97 new matches` under new `main/melee/if/*` units and `97 broken matches` under old `main/melee/un/*` units, indicating rename/path churn rather than real code regression.
The bot also reported mirrored `39 improvements in unmatched functions` and `39 regressions in unmatched functions` across the same old/new unit paths.

Struct relocation used `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct un_80304138_objalloc_t_x8) == 0x20)` and `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct un_80304138_objalloc_t) == 0x20)` in `if/types.h` to preserve layout confidence after moving type definitions.
Minor formatting changes in game-mode files were incidental and should not be interpreted as matching tactics without stronger evidence.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was the automated decomp-dev bot report for GALE01, showing the expected match accounting churn from moving units from `main/melee/un/*` to `main/melee/if/*`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename remaining `un_80...` functions inside `if/textlib.c` or `if/textdraw.c` to descriptive `DevText` or interface names.
Check whether any references to `src/melee/un` or `melee/un` remained after PR 2005.
Look for follow-up PRs that further split `soundtest.h` declarations or rename `if_2FC93.c` to a semantic interface module name.
Audit users of `if/types.h` to ensure they include narrower headers when they only need `DevText` forward declarations.
Compare match reports before and after this rename to distinguish true matching improvements from path-only match movement.
